$sources_analyzed: CDA.swf (4.15 MB compressed / 8.5 MB body, Flash 11, Chrysler Diagnostic Application v6)
config.bin (16-byte hex: E9105EE93BF49D69258AEE921ABC30E9 — likely AES-128 key)

architecture_findings.summary: Both AlfaOBD and CDA are CLIENT applications. Neither contains the routine-ID dispatch catalog as plaintext data in the binary. Both load that catalog at runtime from external sources.

cda_swf.what_it_is: Chrysler Diagnostic Application v6 — the Flash UI for wiTECH 2.
cda_swf.back_end: Calls a wiTECH diagnostic engine via HTTP/SOAP. ECU-specific dispatch (RIDs, security levels, payload framing) lives in the wiTECH SERVER's ODX/CBF database, not in this SWF.

embedded_data.DefineBinaryData_blocks: 9 blocks, all Adobe Pixel Bender shaders (graphic blend effects: ColorDodge, Hue, Saturation, etc.) — NOT dispatch tables
embedded_data.ABC_bytecode: 2 frames totaling 7.3 MB — UI logic + service client orchestration only

alfaobd_exe.dispatch_storage: External SQLite .db (uses Entity Framework 6 SQLite provider per app.config). The .db is the catalog — likely encrypted with the 16-byte hex key in config.bin (E9105EE93BF49D69258AEE921ABC30E9).

embedded_data.DotNet_resources: 14 .resources blobs — all are UI bitmaps (manufacturer logos, icons, blend modes) — no dispatch data
embedded_data.Encrypted_strings: 2079 strings in #US heap, all Dotfuscator-XOR-encrypted (algorithm reversed — see below). Decrypted set is mostly CAN message symbolic names (CAN Output (BCM_A3), CAN Input (SKREEM_A1)) and config DID labels (VehConfig 1-8, Cust.Prog.Features, etc) — no routine IDs.

dotfuscator_decryption.algorithm: per-char XOR; key = 0x6DDC67B5 + salt_arg; key++ per byte; byte-swap output
dotfuscator_decryption.decryption_tool_status: PORTED — alfaobd_decrypt.py works against any future AlfaOBD build with this algo

concrete_uds_extractions.$note: These are the ONLY plaintext UDS byte sequences I found embedded as literal strings or labels in either binary.

BCM_PROXI_read.source: CDA.swf string: 'The Proxi String is read from the BCM using command 222023.'

BCM_PROXI_write.prerequisites_likely: extendedDiagnosticSession (0x10 0x03), SecurityAccess unlock, possibly SGW unlock on 2018+

sgw_unlock_flow_from_cda.summary: CDA implements the Stellantis Authenticated Diagnostics (AD) flow for SGW-equipped vehicles. Auth is via login to Stellantis backend + dongle hardware.
user_flow_strings: Authenticated Diagnostics is required for any vehicle equipped with a Security Gateway (SGW)
Login to Stellantis US and unlock the SGW
If this vehicle is equipped with an SGW and you skip the authentication step, the flash may fail
Unlocking an ADA requires authentication. Click the button below to authenticate...

dongle_pin_handling.purpose: User enters PIN from physical dongle hardware → CDA sends to Stellantis backend → backend issues SGW unlock token

alfaobd_decrypted_string_categories.$note: Categorical summary of 2078 Dotfuscator-decrypted strings — see all_decrypted.json for full set

numeric_label_IDs.note: These are AlfaOBD's internal procedure-table indices, NOT UDS routine identifiers. They map to the DIAG_NAMES descriptive labels in the corrupted .db (alreadyextracted into alfaobdData.generated.js).

tier1_routine_ids_searched.found_in_alfaobd_exe: ZERO. The Tier-1 routine IDs from the shopping list do NOT appear in either binary's strings or numeric constants. The exe doesn't carry the catalog.

uds_complete_reference_summary.$note: Generic UDS reference document — useful as SRT Lab documentation but contains no FCA-specific dispatch data
valuable_content: Full SID 0x00-0xFF allocation map with positive/negative response codes
Diagnostic Session table (0x01..0x7E) with FCA-specific session range 0x40-0x5F flagged
DTC status byte bit map (testFailed, pendingDTC, confirmedDTC, warningIndicatorRequested, etc.)
uds_complete_reference_summary.explicit_disclaimer_from_doc: 'individual DIDs, routines, security levels, seed/key algorithms, programming preconditions, and supported services vary by ECU, model year, software level, market, and network gateway configuration' — author flags that catalog data must come from FCA service info / wiTECH ODX/CBF, not from this PDF.
uds_complete_reference_summary.use_for_srt_lab: Drop the PDF into docs/ as `docs/uds-protocol-reference.pdf` — it's a clean general UDS reference that can be linked from the seed-key calculator + raw UDS console pages for end-user reference.

primary_source_authoritative.location: wiTECH backend server — ODX-D (ISO 22901) or CBF (Common Bus File) databases
primary_source_authoritative.format: FCA proprietary, hundreds of MB to multi-GB per vehicle line
primary_source_authoritative.access: Requires wiTECH 2 license + Stellantis backend account
primary_source_authoritative.rationale_why_not_in_clients: Stellantis updates the catalog continuously without pushing new client binaries — clients always fetch latest

alfaobd_subset_source.location: Local SQLite .db in AlfaOBD install directory (encrypted)
alfaobd_subset_source.decryption_key_candidate: config.bin contents — E9105EE93BF49D69258AEE921ABC30E9 (16 bytes, AES-128 length)
alfaobd_subset_source.note: Previously a 'corrupted' decrypted version yielded DIAG_NAMES labels in alfaobdData.generated.js — confirms the .db is the catalog source. A clean copy is needed.

bench_capture_source.alternative: J2534 PassThru logging during a real procedure run
bench_capture_source.tools: Drew Tech, Cardaq, Tactrix, Kvaser, PCAN, ValueCAN, AlfaOBD's own session log
bench_capture_source.yield_per_capture: Full UDS frame sequence for one procedure including session, security, RID, payload

tier1_routines_updated.$status: Same scaffold as alfaobd-tier1-routines.json — fields below are now informed by CDA architecture findings
tier1_routines_updated.$summary: CDA.swf confirms FCA gates these behind extendedDiagnosticSession + (on SGW vehicles) Stellantis AD unlock. CDA delegates the actual byte construction to the wiTECH engine, so the RID bytes themselves are NOT in CDA.swf. AlfaOBD's encrypted .db is the next-best source.
additional_pre_conditions_now_known_from: 2018+ vehicles: SGW unlock REQUIRED before any of these routines (Stellantis AD)
Bench mode: some procedures can bypass SGW via dongle PIN (DongleSecurityGatewayMessage)
Flash routines (1126, 1520, 1750, 1751) follow the 10-phase template in UDS_Reference §8

files_emitted: alfaobd_decrypt.py — Working Dotfuscator decryptor for any AlfaOBD .NET binary
all_decrypted.json — 2078 decrypted strings from AlfaOBD v2.5.7.0
alfaobd-tier1-routines.json — Tier-1 routine JSON scaffold with confirmed structural fields
this file — comprehensive cross-source extraction summary

next_request_for_user.highest_value: AlfaOBD's external SQLite .db (decryptable with the config.bin AES key + algorithm from the AlfaOBD.exe)
next_request_for_user.alternatively: One bench capture of an FCA UDS procedure (any of the Tier-1 routines) — gives us that routine's exact RID, security level, and payload immediately
